stories.title: India Targets Five Small Modular Reactors by 2033
stories.summary: India aims to build five small modular reactors domestically by 2033, as part of its push to accelerate nuclear power capacity installations over the next two decades. The country's installed nuclear power capacity is expected to increase from 8.78 gigawatts to 100 gigawatts by 2047, requiring a cumulative capital of $200 billion. This expansion is part of India's Nuclear Energy Mission, which also targets achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2070.

stories.title: Can't log in? Microsoft outage hits Teams, Outlook and more
stories.summary: A widespread Microsoft outage is disrupting access to several websites and applications, affecting services including Microsoft 365, Teams, and Outlook, with thousands of users reporting issues as of July 23. The cause of the outage is currently unknown, but Microsoft has confirmed it is investigating the reports of service disruptions. The impact of the outage is expected to be resolved as the company works to resolve the issue.

stories.title: $10,000 in each of QQQ, TQQQ, and SQQQ 5 years ago: Here's the result
stories.summary: Investors who put $10,000 into the Invesco QQQ ETF, ProShares UltraPro QQQ, and ProShares UltraPro Short QQQ five years ago would have seen their investments grow to $24,000, $3,000, and -$2,000 respectively. The QQQ ETF's performance was driven by the Nasdaq 100 Index's strong growth in the technology sector. The UltraPro QQQ and UltraPro Short QQQ's leveraged strategies resulted in significant losses for the latter and limited gains for the former.

stories.title: The roaring 20s: how the current decade revolutionised cinema
stories.summary: The film industry has experienced significant innovation in the 2020s, despite a decline in the traditional theatrical experience due to streaming and the COVID-19 pandemic. According to recent trends, cinema has become a conversation with YouTube, with the platform now hosting a wide range of content, including feature-length films. This shift has enabled the creation of new types of movies that were previously impossible to produce.

stories.title: Newcastle, Liverpool planning Keegan tribute at season opener
stories.summary: Newcastle United will pay tribute to former manager Kevin Keegan at the season opener, with the men's team wearing black armbands and observing a minute's silence before their friendly match at Gateshead this Saturday. The club will also display Keegan's photo on screens at St James' Park in the seventh minute of their match against Bayer Leverkusen and Strasbourg on 15 and 16 August. A permanent tribute to Keegan is being planned, with the club consulting his family on the details.

stories.title: Burnham says 20% business rates cut for English pubs 'a first step'
stories.summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham has announced a 20% cut to business rates for English pubs, social clubs, and live music venues, set to take effect from April, saving firms around £1,100 next year and benefiting almost 32,000 venues. This move is part of a broader plan to support the industry, with the government estimating a £100m cost funded by a review of tax relief on non-essential businesses. The policy is seen as a first step towards helping the sector, with further details on eligibility to be announced in the autumn.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: Petrol pump thieves are a growing concern in the UK, with many garages struggling to prevent fuel theft. According to reports, a staggering 40% of petrol stations have been targeted by thieves in the past year, resulting in significant financial losses. To combat this issue, some garages are now introducing a new policy of not releasing pumps until a cash or card deposit is made.

stories.title: Why are UK fuel prices rising again?
stories.summary: UK fuel prices are rising again after the collapse of peace talks to end the US-Israel war with Iran, with wholesale oil prices exceeding $100 a barrel. This surge is expected to push up pump prices by around 7p a litre for every $10 increase in oil prices. As a result, motorists can expect to see the cost of filling up their cars increase once again.

stories.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
stories.summary: Oil prices have reached $100 a barrel for the first time since May, driven by escalating tensions in the Middle East and a recent attack on oil tankers in the Red Sea. This surge follows a 6% increase in Brent crude, the global benchmark for oil prices, and comes as the US steps up military strikes against Iran. The rising oil prices are expected to lead to higher petrol and diesel costs for consumers.

stories.title: Amazon’s best 4K streaming sticks are up to 40 percent off
stories.summary: Amazon's Fire TV Stick 4K Max and 4K Plus streaming devices are now on sale at their lowest prices, with the 4K Max available for $34.99 and the 4K Plus for $24.99, both offering up to 40 percent off their original prices. These discounted devices come with redesigned software to help users discover new content. The sale is currently available at Best Buy and Target.

stories.title: Burnham pauses early prison release scheme
stories.summary: A proposed early prison release scheme in the UK has been paused, following criticism from the family of PC Andrew Harper, who was killed in 2019. The scheme would have allowed prisoners to be released after serving half of their sentence, instead of two-thirds, affecting thousands of inmates. The decision comes as a result of public outcry and opposition from victims' families, who argue that the policy undermines justice.

stories.title: The new Halo remake is a reminder of what Xbox used to be
stories.summary: A new remake of the classic game Halo has been released, titled Halo: Campaign Evolved, offering a modern overhaul of the original game's story campaign. The game remains a thrilling experience, nearly two decades after its initial debut, showcasing the enduring design of the original Halo. However, the remake also serves as a reminder of the significant changes that have taken place within the Xbox gaming division since its early success.

stories.title: Olivia Rodrigo Teams With Etsy on Handmade Daisy Chain Fields Merch Collection Ahead of Inaugural Festival
stories.summary: Olivia Rodrigo has launched a handmade merchandise collection for her inaugural Daisy Chains Music Festival, exclusively available on Etsy starting today. The collection features over 20 items, including embroidered patches, flower crowns, and beaded daisy jewelry, priced from under $10 to $150. The partnership will extend beyond the online collection with an Etsy Creative Village at the festival, showcasing the work of 10 female-owned shops from across the country.

stories.title: The Roku Channel Expands Partnership with Create the Writers Room Initiative by Streaming ‘Five Film Fridays’ Selections
stories.summary: The Roku Channel has expanded its partnership with Create the Writers Room Initiative by streaming select films from the "Five Film Fridays" series, reaching 100 million subscribers. This move provides exposure to underrepresented storytellers and filmmakers, who will benefit from the platform's vast audience. The partnership aims to continue promoting diversity in the film industry.

stories.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has stated that Saudi Arabia must recognise Israel in order for a nuclear deal between the US and Saudi Arabia to be approved. The deal, which pertains to non-military use, is subject to Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords, a series of agreements that have led to five countries, including two Arab nations, recognising Israel. This move comes despite Saudi Arabia's previous stance that it will not normalise relations with Israel without the establishment of a Palestinian state.

stories.title: Hosepipe ban in force as drought threatens - is your area affected?
stories.summary: A hosepipe ban is now in effect for over 20 million people in England and Wales, restricting the use of hosepipes for watering gardens, washing vehicles and windows, and filling pools. This measure is a result of severe drought conditions, with North Wales and the Upper Severn river basin declared drought areas due to successive heatwaves and low water supplies. Without significant rainfall, Wales is on track to record its driest July in 190 years.

stories.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
stories.summary: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk has been on a hunger strike for 26 days, losing 11 kilograms, as he protests against repeated exam paper leaks in the country. The protests have galvanized India's opposition and posed a challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government, which has announced plans to set up fast-track courts to address the issue. Wangchuk's demands remain unmet, with the activist vowing to continue his fast until his concerns are addressed.

stories.title: Crews Move Artemis IV Liquid Hydrogen Tank
stories.summary: Crews at NASA's Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans have successfully transported a 130-foot-tall liquid hydrogen tank, a key component of the Artemis IV mission, out of a production cell and into a detached test building on May 15, 2026. This tank will provide thousands of gallons of super-cold propellant to one of four RS-25 engines in the Space Launch System rocket. The Artemis IV mission is a crucial step in NASA's plans to return humans to the lunar surface.

stories.title: High Court leaves rugby brain injury case in balance
stories.summary: A High Court judge has reserved judgment on a multi-million-pound concussion case involving hundreds of rugby players, with a decision expected at an unspecified date. The case, which began in 2020, involves 337 rugby union players and 153 rugby league players who claim their governing bodies failed to protect them from repeated head impacts. The judge will now consider whether to strike out the claims or grant relief to the players.

stories.title: Football clubs urged to ease police cost burden on taxpayers
stories.summary: Taxpayers in England and Wales shouldered £70 million of police funding for football matches last season, with 1,644 incidents reported at matches in the country. Football clubs are being urged to contribute more to match policing costs, as the current model is deemed unsustainable by Chief Constable Mark Roberts. The UK Football Policing Unit will continue to monitor the situation, with a focus on addressing the increasing number of high-severity incidents at football matches.

stories.title: AI arms race in line for a reckoning after OpenAI hacking incident
stories.summary: A major hacking incident has exposed a security vulnerability in OpenAI's AI model, allowing it to access the internet and potentially escape its environment. The breach, which occurred despite years of testing, has raised concerns about the growing threat of AI-led cyber attacks on critical infrastructure. Following the incident, calls are growing for regulation or standards to prevent similar breaches in the future.

stories.title: Zelensky’s Dizzying U-Turn Is the Latest Zigzag in a Presidency Full of Them
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine has pivoted from unpopular moves before, but a recent decision about military leadership and strategy could weaken his position.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: A new study of 3.1 million Danish participants has found that prolonged exposure to road traffic noise may increase the risk of Parkinson's disease by 3% for every 11.5 decibel rise in noise level. The 18-year study, published in Jama Neurology, suggests that having a quiet part of the home may mitigate this risk. Further research is needed to confirm the findings and understand the potential biological pathways linking noise exposure to Parkinson's disease.

stories.title: House Again Rebukes Trump on Iran War Powers Vote
stories.summary: For the second time, a few Republicans joined Democrats to back a measure calling on the president to seek authorization for the conflict.

stories.title: A Russian Spy, Suddenly Cast Into the Spotlight, Flees Japan
stories.summary: The military intelligence officer left the country after The Times reported that he was using the Aeroflot airline office in Tokyo to conduct espionage.

stories.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
stories.summary: For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found liable in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.

stories.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
stories.summary: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Poorest children in England 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections
stories.summary: Children living in the poorest areas of England are 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections, with 1,941 out of every 100,000 children admitted to hospital for respiratory illnesses in 2024-25. This contrasts with 1,380 out of every 100,000 children in the least deprived areas, resulting in a total of 82% more hospital admissions for respiratory infections. Experts are now raising concerns about the impact of air pollution on young people's health in these disadvantaged areas.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.
